name: Grandma Black's Scrapple
recipeInstructions:
1. Simmer the pork in boiling water with 2 teaspoons of salt until it is very tender (about 2 hours). Save the stock.
2. Shred the cooked pork into fine pieces or threads using a fork.
3. Bring 1 quart of the saved stock to a boil.
4. Mix the cornmeal and cold water, then add to the boiling stock, stirring continuously to prevent clumps.
5. Cook until thickened.
6. Add seasonings and stir in the shredded pork to coat evenly with the cornmeal mush. Cook for an additional 5 minutes.
7. Pour the mixture into a buttered loaf pan (9x5x3 inches).
8. Chill until firm, then slice into 1/4- to 1/2-inch thick pieces.
9. Dip each slice in flour to lightly coat, then brown on both sides in hot oil.
10. Serve hot with plenty of maple syrup. Some people prefer it plain or with butter; my brother liked his with ketchup.
